l1: What is the exact issue being reported?
Is the problem related to: ( Cloud PC not visible? / Sign-in failure? /Connection failure? / Black screen? / Performance issue? / Disconnection? / Provisioning issue?)
How many users are impacted (single, multiple, all)?
When did the issue first occur? Can the user access the Cloud PC through -> (Windows App? / Web Portal (windows365.microsoft.com))?
Is the issue reproducible?
Does it occur every time or intermittently?
Does it occur on multiple devices?
Need to collect the basic information -> ( Cloud PC name / User UPN / Tenant ID / Timestamp with timezone / Screenshot of error / Correlation ID / Session ID)
l2: What type of license customer is using ? If License assigned successfully?
Is the Provisioning Policy assigned?
Cloud PC state (Provisioned / Grace Period / Failed / Unavailable)?
Is Cloud PC visible in Intune?
Is this Enterprise, Frontline Dedicated, or Frontline Shared Cloud PC? Check if this Entra ID join or Hybrid Join
What is the Network location of user
What is the Cloud PC region
What is the RTT latency
Is there any VPN/Proxy involvement
What is the session type whether it is UDP or TCP
Is the RDP Shortpath is enabled or not
What is the Windows App version
What is the AVD Host App version
Check the status of Intune policy
Check the device compliance status

l1: Can you see the Cloud PC in Windows 365 Portal?
Did you recently receive a Cloud PC license?
Was the Cloud PC working previously?
Does the issue happen before launching the Cloud PC? Or During sign-in? Or After desktop loads?
Can you access through another network?
Any authentication prompt?
MFA challenge failing?
Recent password change?
Is the session disconnecting?
Any lag or latency?
Is the Cloud PC frozen or black screen?
l2: Was provisioning completed successfully?
Any provisioning failures in CPCD?
Is ANC validation successful?
Any Azure infrastructure dependency failures?
Client Telemetry Investigation -> Correlation ID available? / Session ID available? / Activity ID available?
Any W365 App telemetry failures?
Any Graph API failures?
Any token acquisition failures?
Check if the RTT values exceeding 200 ms?
User and CPC located in different regions?
Check if UDP disconnections observed?
If this is Windows 365 boot device then check if the Boot-to-Cloud policy applied?
Check the Registry values present -> BootToCloudMode = 1 / OverrideShellProgram = 1 / SharedPC Mode enabled
Is Windows App launch successful?

l1: Verify license assignment. In Microsoft 365 Admin Center → Users → Active Users, confirm the user has a Windows 365 license (Business or Enterprise).
Verify user assignment. In Windows 365 Admin Portal, check that the Cloud PC is provisioned for the user.
Validate Cloud PC appears in Windows 365 Portal -> Go to https://windows365.microsoft.com and confirm the Cloud PC appears under the users account.
Windows App -> Install the Windows App and verify the Cloud PC is listed.
Intune (if admin available)-> In Endpoint Manager → Devices, confirm the Cloud PC is enrolled and compliant.
Test access from-> Web Client / Windows App / Alternate network / Alternate device
Collect the details -> Cloud PC Name / Correlation ID / Session ID / Screenshot / Timestamp / Tenant ID
l2: Validate provisioning events -> Go to Endpoint Manager → Devices → Cloud PCs → Provisioning status. Confirm events show Succeeded.
Validate ANC health -> Check Azure Network Connection (ANC) health in Endpoint Manager. Ensure connectivity tests (DNS, domain join, latency) pass.
Check CPC Availability Dashboard -> In Windows 365 Admin Portal, review the availability dashboard for Cloud PCs across tenants.
Confirm Cloud PCs show Available and not Failed or Provisioning.
In Azure Portal, check the underlying VM resource health (CPU, memory, disk).
In Windows 365 diagnostics, check user connection attempts and outcomes
Use diagnostics to confirm Cloud PC traffic is flowing over optimal UDP Shortpath; fallback to TCP if needed.
Measure round-trip time (RTT) in diagnostics to confirm latency is within acceptable limits.
Ensure Cloud PCs are provisioned in the correct region closest to the user for performance.

l1: Collect the Error message / Correlation ID / Time stamp /Screenshot
MSRD Collect
Gather client-side logs from the Windows App
l2: Review CPCD diagnostics -> Inn Windows 365 Admin Portal → Cloud PC diagnostics, review CPCD (Cloud PC Control Diagnostics) logs for provisioning errors.
Review provisioning activity ID -> Each provisioning attempt generates an Activity ID. Capture it from the portal or error logs for troubleshooting.
In Endpoint Manager, view Cloud PC restart/recovery logs to ensure no repeated failures.
